description: Career-based course recommendations for TCU Kinesiology majors. Used to replace generic TCU Core placeholders and free electives with courses that best prepare students for their intended career path.

Pre-PT/OT/DPT.label: Pre-Physical or Occupational Therapy (DPT/OT)
Pre-PT/OT/DPT.description: Optimizes plan for doctorate-level PT or OT programs. Prioritizes biology, chemistry, physics, psychology, and statistics.

freeElectiveRecommendations.title: Organic Chemistry I
freeElectiveRecommendations.notes: Required by most DPT and OT programs

freeElectiveRecommendations.title: Organic Chemistry II
freeElectiveRecommendations.notes: Required by most DPT programs

freeElectiveRecommendations.title: Abnormal Psychology
freeElectiveRecommendations.notes: Recommended for OT applicants

freeElectiveRecommendations.title: Developmental Psychology
freeElectiveRecommendations.notes: Highly valued by OT programs

freeElectiveRecommendations.title: Nutrition
freeElectiveRecommendations.notes: Recommended for health professional applications

freeElectiveRecommendations.title: Genetics
freeElectiveRecommendations.notes: Strengthens science background for grad school

freeElectiveRecommendations.title: First Aid and CPR
freeElectiveRecommendations.notes: Required clinical prerequisite

freeElectiveRecommendations.title: Exercise Assessment and Prescription
freeElectiveRecommendations.notes: Directly applicable to PT/OT clinical practice

Pre-PT/OT/DPT.advisingNote: Most DPT programs require observation hours (100+ hrs recommended). OT programs require volunteer/shadowing experience. Plan to take the GRE by junior year.

Pre-Med/DO/PA.label: Pre-Medicine, DO, or Physician Assistant
Pre-Med/DO/PA.description: Optimizes plan for medical school (MD/DO) or PA programs. Prioritizes biology, chemistry, biochemistry, physics, and statistics.

freeElectiveRecommendations.title: Organic Chemistry I
freeElectiveRecommendations.notes: Required by all MD/DO/PA programs

freeElectiveRecommendations.title: Organic Chemistry II
freeElectiveRecommendations.notes: Required by all MD/DO/PA programs

freeElectiveRecommendations.title: Biochemistry I
freeElectiveRecommendations.notes: Required or strongly recommended by most programs; MCAT tested

freeElectiveRecommendations.title: Genetics
freeElectiveRecommendations.notes: Required or strongly recommended; MCAT tested

freeElectiveRecommendations.title: Cell Biology
freeElectiveRecommendations.notes: Recommended for MD/DO applicants

freeElectiveRecommendations.title: Introduction to Psychology
freeElectiveRecommendations.notes: MCAT tested — Psychology and Social Sciences section

freeElectiveRecommendations.title: Introduction to Sociology
freeElectiveRecommendations.notes: MCAT tested — Psychology and Social Sciences section

freeElectiveRecommendations.title: Nutrition
freeElectiveRecommendations.notes: Recommended for PA applicants

freeElectiveRecommendations.title: Elementary Statistics
freeElectiveRecommendations.notes: Required by many PA programs; replaces MATH 10043 if not already taken

Pre-Med/DO/PA.advisingNote: MCAT preparation should begin no later than spring of junior year. MD/DO programs require 1000+ clinical hours. PA programs typically require 2000-3000 patient care hours. Join TCU Pre-Health Professions advising early.

Coaching/Strength and Conditioning.label: Coaching or Strength & Conditioning
applicableMajors: Physical Education with Strength and Conditioning
Coaching/Strength and Conditioning.description: Optimizes plan for careers in athletic performance, strength and conditioning, or coaching. Prioritizes biomechanics, exercise physiology, and performance courses.

freeElectiveRecommendations.title: Theory and Practice of Strength Training
freeElectiveRecommendations.notes: Core for CSCS exam preparation

freeElectiveRecommendations.title: Advanced Strength and Conditioning
freeElectiveRecommendations.notes: Advanced CSCS preparation

freeElectiveRecommendations.title: Practicum in Strength and Conditioning
freeElectiveRecommendations.notes: Hands-on experience — highly recommended

freeElectiveRecommendations.title: Nutrition
freeElectiveRecommendations.notes: Sports nutrition knowledge essential for S&C careers

freeElectiveRecommendations.title: Psychology of Sport
freeElectiveRecommendations.notes: Athlete motivation and performance psychology

freeElectiveRecommendations.title: Nutrition and Physical Activity
freeElectiveRecommendations.notes: Applied nutrition for performance

freeElectiveRecommendations.title: Management
freeElectiveRecommendations.notes: Useful for coaching/program management roles

Coaching/Strength and Conditioning.advisingNote: Consider pursuing the CSCS (Certified Strength and Conditioning Specialist) certification through NSCA after graduation. Internship experience with a collegiate or professional program is highly valuable.

Teaching/Physical Education.label: Teaching — Physical Education (K-12)
applicableMajors: Physical Education with Strength and Conditioning
Teaching/Physical Education.description: Optimizes plan for K-12 PE teacher certification. Education sequence is required and structured — limited flexibility in electives.

freeElectiveRecommendations.title: Introduction to Education
freeElectiveRecommendations.notes: Required — take early

freeElectiveRecommendations.title: Special Education
freeElectiveRecommendations.notes: Recommended for all PE teachers — inclusive classrooms

freeElectiveRecommendations.title: Psychology of Sport
freeElectiveRecommendations.notes: Valuable for motivating K-12 students

freeElectiveRecommendations.title: First Aid and CPR
freeElectiveRecommendations.notes: Required for most school districts

freeElectiveRecommendations.title: Physical Activity and Disability
freeElectiveRecommendations.notes: Adapted PE — valuable for inclusive teaching

Teaching/Physical Education.advisingNote: Texas teacher certification requires passing the TExES PE 7-12 exam. Student teaching (EDUC 40966, 6 credits) is typically completed in the final semester. Apply to the College of Education teacher preparation program by sophomore year.

Sport and Exercise Psychology.label: Sport & Exercise Psychology (Grad School Path)
Sport and Exercise Psychology.description: Optimizes plan for graduate programs in sport psychology, counseling, or performance psychology. Prioritizes psychology coursework alongside kinesiology.

freeElectiveRecommendations.title: Abnormal Psychology
freeElectiveRecommendations.notes: Highly recommended for counseling/psychology graduate programs

freeElectiveRecommendations.title: Developmental Psychology
freeElectiveRecommendations.notes: Valuable context for working with athletes at various stages

freeElectiveRecommendations.title: Mental Performance Consulting
freeElectiveRecommendations.notes: Directly aligned with sport psych career path

freeElectiveRecommendations.title: Psychology of Aging and Physical Activity
freeElectiveRecommendations.notes: Broadens scope for exercise psychology practice

freeElectiveRecommendations.title: Psychology of Injury in Physical Activity
freeElectiveRecommendations.notes: Critical for working with injured athletes

freeElectiveRecommendations.title: Elementary Statistics
freeElectiveRecommendations.notes: Required for most graduate programs in psychology

freeElectiveRecommendations.title: Introduction to Sociology
freeElectiveRecommendations.notes: Useful context for understanding group dynamics in sport

Sport and Exercise Psychology.advisingNote: Graduate programs in sport psychology (MS or PhD) typically require GRE scores and research experience. Seek a research assistantship or independent study with a TCU faculty member by junior year. Consider the CMPC (Certified Mental Performance Consultant) credential pathway.

Clinical/Allied Health.label: Clinical or Health and Wellness Professional (non-MD)
Clinical/Allied Health.description: Optimizes plan for careers in clinical exercise physiology, cardiac rehab, corporate wellness, or allied health. Prioritizes health science, nutrition, and clinical coursework.

freeElectiveRecommendations.title: Nutrition
freeElectiveRecommendations.notes: Essential for clinical exercise and wellness roles

freeElectiveRecommendations.title: First Aid and CPR
freeElectiveRecommendations.notes: Required for most clinical positions

freeElectiveRecommendations.title: Exercise Assessment and Prescription
freeElectiveRecommendations.notes: Core clinical skill

freeElectiveRecommendations.title: Introduction to Psychology
freeElectiveRecommendations.notes: Useful for patient communication and behavior change

freeElectiveRecommendations.title: Management
freeElectiveRecommendations.notes: Helpful for wellness program management roles

freeElectiveRecommendations.title: Genetics
freeElectiveRecommendations.notes: Strengthens science background for clinical settings

Clinical/Allied Health.advisingNote: Consider pursuing the ACSM-EP (Exercise Physiologist) certification or CEP (Clinical Exercise Physiologist) after graduation. ACE and NSCA certifications are also widely recognized in wellness and fitness settings.

Undecided.label: Undecided / Exploring Options
applicableMajors: Physical Education with Strength and Conditioning
Undecided.description: Uses balanced general education courses. AI will ask the student about career interests to refine future plans.

freeElectiveRecommendations.title: Nutrition
freeElectiveRecommendations.notes: Broadly useful across all kinesiology career paths

freeElectiveRecommendations.title: First Aid and CPR
freeElectiveRecommendations.notes: Useful regardless of career path

freeElectiveRecommendations.title: Introduction to Psychology
freeElectiveRecommendations.notes: Broadly applicable

freeElectiveRecommendations.title: Management
freeElectiveRecommendations.notes: Useful for any leadership or administrative role

Undecided.advisingNote: No career goal selected. The plan uses balanced general education defaults. Consider meeting with a TCU advisor or exploring the career tracks available in FrogForward to get a more tailored plan.
